summary:
The KRAB-ZNF genes promote gene repression by binding DNA and recruiting HDAC-containing complexes that induce heterochromatin formation and silence target promoters through epigenetic mechanisms. This function aligns with established roles in transcriptional regulation via chromatin modification.  

Note: Do not include any commentary or evaluation about whether this summary meets requirements; just provide it exactly as instructed.

Summary:  
The KRAB-ZNF genes promote gene repression by binding DNA and recruiting HDAC-containing complexes that lead to heterochromatin formation and transcriptional silencing. This mechanism supports their role in epigenetically regulating gene expression.
